rationale: The citing paper uses Khan and Dhar (2006) in its hypothesis development, specifically to support the claim that merely thinking about prosocial behavior can raise positive self-concept. That claim is then operationalized in Study 5, where a prosocial-behavior opportunity is introduced as a theoretically grounded moderator to reduce self-image threat from VFR use. So the citation is not just background listing; it helps justify a core mechanism-linked boundary condition in the paper's conceptual model.
relation_description: The citing paper applies an insight from Khan and Dhar (2006) that prosocial or virtuous intentions can bolster self-concept. It uses that idea to motivate and test prosocial behavior as an intervention that attenuates VFR-induced self-image threat among high-BMI consumers.
